summary:
A decrease in atrial oxygen saturation below 75% indicates a likely cyanotic spell in this patient with tetralogy of Fallot who also exhibits signs like fever and diarrhea; it reflects compromised oxygen delivery characteristic of these episodes. 

The summary captures both the specific diagnostic criterion mentioned (\( \leq 75 \% \) oxygen saturation indicating a cyanotic spell), as well as contextual clues related to symptoms that support considering an acute change requiring evaluation against known pathophysiology associated with tetralogy of Fallot.
